events.place: Church of the Friar Preachers of the Blessed Virgin and Saint Dominic of Perth, Perthshire, Scotland/Stabbed to death by plot of Sir Robert Graham at Blackfriars' Priory at Perth

events.name: She and Louis of Savoy, her first husband, separated, divorced, and their marriage was annulled upon the request of Charles VII of France.

She was legally divorced from George Gordon on the ground of consanguinity with his first wife, Elizabeth Dunbar, 8th Countess of Murray, as the two ladies were within the third and fourth degrees of relation.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland於1432年出生在蘇格蘭培爾培斯時，她的父親James I Stewart King of Scotland38歲，母親Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland25歲。她於1460年3月10日與Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1494年10月13日在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾塔易隆安。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Annabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabell
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabelle
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland was born in 1432, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, James I Stewart King of Scotland, was 38 and her mother, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, was 25. She married Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland on 10 March 1460, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 13 October 1494, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Tayinloan, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland est née en 1432, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, James I Stewart King of Scotland, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland le 10 mars 1460, à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 13 octobre 1494, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Tayinloan, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland 1432 in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James I Stewart King of Scotland 38 und ihre Mutter Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland 25. Sie hat am 10. März 1460 in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. Oktober 1494 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Tayinloan, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland è nata il nel 1432 nel Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, sua padre, James I Stewart King of Scotland, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland il 10 marzo 1460, nel Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 13 ottobre 1494, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Tayinloan, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotlandが1432年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで生まれた時，父James I Stewart King of Scotlandは38歳，母Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotlandは25歳であった。1460年3月10日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンでSir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1494年10月13日にイギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで62歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルティイインロアンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland(이)가 1432년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James I Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1460년 3월 10일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1494년 10월 13일 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 타이인로안에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland родилась в 1432 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени James I Stewart King of Scotland было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland 10 марта 1460 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 13 октября 1494 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности теиинлоан, аргилл, Шотландия.
